topic: Claude Sonnet 5 vs GPT-5.4 for Coding: SWE-bench Benchmark Comparison 2026

key_points: Claude Sonnet 5 (codenamed 'Fennec') released February 3, 2026
92.4% on SWE-bench Verified — highest of any model at launch
2M token context window and adaptive thinking architecture
Outperforms Claude Opus 4.6 (80.8%) despite mid-tier price point
88.3% on OSWorld-Verified, beating human expert baseline of 72.4%
SWE-bench Pro vs Verified: Pro has 1,865 multi-language tasks vs Verified's 500 Python-only tasks
SWE-bench Verified is potentially contaminated — best model 46% on Pro vs 81% on Verified
OpenAI has stopped reporting Verified scores and recommends SWE-Bench Pro
Claude Opus 4.7 leads SWE-bench Pro at 64.3%, GPT-5.4 at 59.1%
SWE-bench Pro requires 10+ lines of change per task; Verified allows 1-2 line trivial fixes
SWE-bench 2026 leaderboard: GPT-5.5 leads at 88.7%, Claude Opus 4.7 at 87.6%
SWE-bench Verified can be gamed via agent scaffolding tuning
Benchmark results should be treated as directional signals, not precise measurements
A model at 80% is almost certainly better than one at 40% but 80% vs 82% is noise
SWE-bench Coding Agent Leaderboard tracks both Claude and GPT models
Claude Code agent outperforms OpenAI Codex CLI with 67% win rate in blind evaluations
Agentic workflows matter as much as raw model scores
Multi-file codebase handling is a key differentiator for Claude
Best AI coding models 2026: Claude and GPT dominate top spots
Claude Sonnet 5: 92.4% SWE-bench Verified; GPT-5.4: 57.7% SWE-bench Pro
70% of developers prefer Claude for coding tasks in 2026
Claude is 2-3x faster for code generation (44-63 tok/s vs GPT's 20-30 tok/s)

stats.fact: Claude Sonnet 5 achieves 92.4% on SWE-bench Verified — highest of any model at launch in 2026
Claude Sonnet 5 initial SWE-bench score: 82.1% (first-reported at launch, February 3, 2026)
GPT-5.4 scores 57.7% on SWE-bench Pro (harder, contamination-resistant benchmark)
GPT-5.4 scores approximately 77-78% on SWE-bench Verified
Claude Code achieves 67% win rate over OpenAI Codex CLI in blind code quality evaluations (early 2026)
70% of developers prefer Claude for coding tasks in 2026, citing multi-file handling and fewer hallucinated API calls
Claude Sonnet 5 generates code at 44-63 tokens/sec vs GPT-5.4's 20-30 tokens/sec (2-3x faster)
Claude Sonnet 5 scores 88.3% on OSWorld-Verified; human experts baseline is 72.4%
Claude Opus 4.7 leads SWE-bench Pro at 64.3%; GPT-5.4 at 59.1% (May 2026)
GPT-5.5 leads SWE-bench Verified in May 2026 at 88.7%; Claude Opus 4.7 at 87.6%
SWE-bench Verified has 500 Python-only tasks; SWE-bench Pro has 1,865 multi-language tasks requiring 10+ lines of change
Claude Sonnet 5 pricing: $3/1M input tokens; GPT-5.4: $2.50/1M input tokens
GPT-5.4 ranks #5 out of 115 models in coding benchmarks with average score of 89.3
GPT-5.4 released March 5, 2026 — first mainline OpenAI model with native computer-use and 1M token context

angles: Benchmark theater vs real-world coding: Why SWE-bench Verified scores can mislead developers (contamination issue)
The Mid-tier upset: Claude Sonnet 5 beats Claude Opus 4.6 at half the cost — is the flagship tier dead?
Speed as a competitive moat: Claude's 2-3x faster token generation changes developer iteration loops
Agentic coding battle: Claude Code's 67% win rate over Codex CLI in real terminal-based tasks
Which tasks favor each model: Claude wins refactoring/debugging, GPT wins boilerplate/scaffolding
The SWE-bench Pro pivot: Why serious engineers should ignore Verified scores and watch Pro instead
Context window comparison: 2M tokens (Sonnet 5) vs 1M tokens (GPT-5.4) for large codebases
